goal: Turn Autoflow from a capable CLI harness into a stable, continuously iterating AI delivery pipeline with recovery, queueing, QA loops, CI gates, and delivery automation.

execution_strategy.planning_rule: Complete T1 before dispatching implementation-heavy work so the backlog, slice sizing, and verification rules are explicit.
execution_strategy.retry_rule: Do not allow implementation retries without structured QA findings, strategy memory context, and a bounded retry count.
execution_strategy.parallelism_rule: Do not run more than one task per role until queue leases and zombie recovery exist in T2 and T4.
execution_strategy.delivery_rule: Do not automate PR creation or merge until T7 and T8 pass with green CI and push safeguards.

tasks.title: Compile and normalize the autonomous delivery backlog
acceptance_criteria: The spec backlog contains at least these 9 executable tasks with explicit dependencies, priorities, owner roles, slice sizes, and verification commands.
The backlog is compatible with the current Autoflow state machine and can be exported through the Taskmaster adapter without schema loss.
Each task includes acceptance criteria that are concrete enough for an implementation agent to act on without additional human decomposition.
The active planning run is reflected in task state and notes so the scheduler is not working against stale backlog state.

notes.note: Expanded the single-task backlog into a 9-task roadmap aligned to the current control-plane architecture.
Current active run is 20260306T172243Z-spec-writer-openclaw-autonomy; its prompt should be treated as stale planning context and either completed with refreshed output or replaced.

tasks.title: Implement run leases, heartbeats, and zombie session recovery
acceptance_criteria: Each run writes lease metadata including tmux session id, last heartbeat timestamp, and stale timeout policy.
A stalled or dead tmux session can be detected and reclassified from active to stalled/abandoned without manual file editing.
Autoflow exposes explicit recovery commands such as sweep-runs and recover-run, and workflow-state stops treating stale runs as blockers.
Tests cover normal heartbeat updates, stale-run detection, and safe recovery of an interrupted implementation run.

tasks.title: Implement a bounded QA self-healing loop
acceptance_criteria: A reviewer failure can trigger a bounded fix-and-re-review loop without human prompting between every attempt.
The loop reuses structured findings, resume metadata, and native backend continuation instead of spawning unrelated fresh sessions.
Each QA iteration records findings, summaries, and retry decisions in durable state so the loop can resume after interruption.
Tests cover pass-on-first-review, pass-after-fix, repeated failure until retry cap, and missing-fix-request rejection.

tasks.title: Build a persistent queue and worker-slot scheduler
acceptance_criteria: Autoflow maintains a persistent queue with per-task lease ownership, worker-slot limits, and deduplication for repeated dispatch ticks.
The scheduler can safely run multiple background workers without dispatching the same task twice.
Role-based and backend-based concurrency limits are configurable and visible in coordination-brief output.
Tests cover queue acquisition, lease expiry, duplicate-dispatch prevention, and fallback behavior when no slot is available.

tasks.title: Turn strategy memory into an adaptive policy engine
acceptance_criteria: Repeated failure patterns can automatically influence dispatch policy, verification intensity, or backend/model selection.
Strategy memory can create planner notes or follow-up tasks when recurring categories, hotspots, or retry storms are detected.
The policy engine distinguishes between informational history and actionable strategy changes so prompts do not accumulate noise.
Tests cover policy escalation on repeated findings and verify that success runs do not pollute the failure playbook.

tasks.title: Add a real agent registry with auth and capability health
acceptance_criteria: Health checks distinguish binary presence from actual readiness, including auth state, model flag support, and ACP handshake status where applicable.
The discovered-agent registry persists capability snapshots and feeds dispatch decisions instead of relying only on --help probing.
Dispatch can block or reroute when an agent backend is installed but not actually usable for the required protocol or model.
Tests cover healthy, missing, degraded-auth, and degraded-capability agent states.

tasks.title: Expand CI into end-to-end autonomy gates and scheduled dry-runs
acceptance_criteria: CI validates not only unit tests but also a representative CLI-level end-to-end flow for run creation, completion, retry, and orchestration.
A scheduled dry-run workflow can execute the outer orchestrator in non-destructive mode and upload health/dispatch artifacts for inspection.
The same repo-local check contract is used by local iteration ticks and GitHub Actions so automation cannot bypass CI expectations.
Failures in CI or dry-run workflows produce actionable artifacts rather than only exit codes.

tasks.title: Add command policy, secret scanning, and push safeguards
acceptance_criteria: Configured verification and dispatch commands are validated against an allowlist or policy layer before execution.
Push and commit automation can block on detected secrets, oversized diffs, or risky file classes such as workflows or auth config.
The orchestrator reports security or policy blockers explicitly so scheduled jobs fail safely instead of silently.
Tests cover blocked dangerous commands, secret-detection failures, and safe overrides for explicitly allowed maintenance tasks.

tasks.title: Close the delivery loop with PR automation, cleanup, and metrics
acceptance_criteria: Completed work can automatically prepare draft PR metadata, merge-readiness signals, and cleanup actions for stale worktrees or branches.
Autoflow produces delivery metrics such as cycle time, retry rate, finding categories, and commit granularity for each spec.
Scheduled automation can summarize progress and blockers without needing a human to inspect raw state files.
Tests or dry-run fixtures cover PR metadata generation, stale-worktree cleanup, and metrics aggregation from historical runs.
